The task instruction for the assistant is to write a python function that extracts and counts the number of unique email domains from a list of email addresses the function should be efficient handle valid email addresses in lowercase and return an integer representing the number of unique domains additionally the function must be well documented with a clear description of its purpose parameters and return value the input is a list of email addresses strings for example user1 gmail com user2 yahoo com user3 gmail com user4 outlook com the output is an integer representing the number of unique email domains in the list for the input user1 gmail com user2 yahoo com user3 gmail com user4 outlook com the output should be 3 because there are three unique domains gmail com yahoo com and outlook com the constraints are the function must be well documented with a clear description of its purpose parameters and return value highlight at least 2 sections in your answer using markdown i e highlighted section the function should split each email address at the symbol to extract the domain part highlighted section the function should return the length of the set containing the unique domains which ensures o 1 average time complexity for insertions and lookups the additional notes are assume all email addresses are valid and in lowercase include a clear explanation of how the function works and why the chosen strategy is effective the function should be able to handle large datasets typical in digital advertising analytics the use of set is a niche and domain specific strategy for efficiently removing duplicates in such scenarios  

the function works by splitting each email address at the symbol to extract the domain part this ensures that only the domain is considered for uniqueness the function then stores these domains in a set which automatically removes duplicates the set data structure is used because it provides o 1 average time complexity for insertions and lookups making the function efficient for large datasets the function returns the length of the set which represents the number of unique domains this approach is effective for analyzing customer data in digital advertising campaigns especially when targeting specific regions like asia the function is straightforward and generalizable making it suitable for various applications the use of set ensures that the function handles duplicates efficiently and provides accurate results the function is well documented with clear explanations of its purpose parameters and return value making it easy to understand and use the function is designed to meet the requirements of the task and provide reliable results for the given input format
